Kikuyu: Nĩ niĩ Paũlũ, mũtũmwo wa Kristũ Jesũ nĩ ũndũ wa kwenda kwa Ngai, tũrĩ na Timotheo mũrũ wa Ithe witũ, Twatũma marũa maya kũrĩ kanitha wa Ngai ũrĩa ũrĩ Korinitho, o hamwe na andũ othe aamũre arĩa marĩ Akaia guothe:
English: Paul, an apostle of Christ† Jesus through the will of God, and Timothy our brother, to the assembly of God which is at Corinth, with all the saints who are in the whole of Achaia:

Kikuyu: Wega na thayũ kuuma kũrĩ Ngai Ithe witũ o na Mwathani Jesũ Kristũ iroikara na inyuĩ.
English: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

Kikuyu: Ngai na Ithe wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ arogoocwo, o we Ithe witũ wa tha, na Ngai mwene ũhoro wothe wa kũũmanĩrĩria,
English: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and God of all comfort,

Kikuyu: ũrĩa ũtũũmagĩrĩria mathĩĩna-inĩ maitũ mothe, nĩguo na ithuĩ tũhotage kũhooreragia arĩa marĩ na thĩĩna o wothe na ũndũ wa kũũmĩrĩrio kũrĩa ithuĩ ene tũũmĩrĩirio nakuo nĩ Ngai.
English: who comforts us in all our affliction, that we may be able to comfort those who are in any affliction, through the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o ta ũrĩa kũnyariirwo gwa Kristũ kũingĩhĩte thĩinĩ witũ-rĩ, ũguo noguo kũũmanĩrĩria gwitũ kũingĩhĩte nĩ ũndũ wa Kristũ.
English: For as the sufferings of Christ abound to us, even so our comfort also abounds through Christ.

Kikuyu: Tũngĩthĩĩnio-rĩ, tũthĩĩnagio nĩguo mũũmĩrĩrio na mũhonokio; tũngĩũmĩrĩrio-rĩ, tũũmagĩrĩrio nĩguo na inyuĩ mũgĩe na ũũmĩrĩru, ũrĩa ũciaraga gũkirĩrĩria mũkĩnyariirwo na mathĩĩna o ta marĩa tũthĩĩnagio namo.
English: But if we are afflicted, it is for your comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is for your comfort, which produces in you the patient enduring of the same sufferings which we also suffer.

Kikuyu: Nakĩo kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro kĩrĩa tũrĩ nakĩo nĩ ũndũ wanyu nĩ kĩrũmu, tondũ nĩtũũĩ atĩ o ta ũrĩa mũgwatanagĩra na ithuĩ maũndũ-inĩ marĩa tũnyariiragwo namo-rĩ, no taguo mũgwatanagĩra na ithuĩ ũhoro-inĩ wa kũũmanĩrĩria o naguo.
English: Our hope for you is steadfast, knowing that, since you are partakers of the sufferings, so you are also of the comfort.

Kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, tũtikwenda mũkorwo mũtooĩ mathĩĩna marĩa twarĩ namo bũrũri wa Asia. Twarĩ hatĩka-inĩ nene mũno makĩria ma ũrĩa tũngĩahotire gũkirĩrĩria, nginya ha ũhoro witũ wa gũtũũra muoyo tũkĩyũũmĩra tha.
English: For we don’t desire to have you uninformed, brothers,‡ concerning our affliction which happened to us in Asia: that we were weighed down exceedingly, beyond our power, so much that we despaired even of life.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ, ngoro-inĩ ciitũ twaiguaga tũtuĩrĩirwo gũkua. No kwahanĩkire ũguo nĩguo tũtikae kwĩĩhoka ithuĩ ene, no twĩhoke Ngai, ũrĩa ũriũkagia arĩa akuũ.
English: Yes, we ourselves have had the sentence of death within ourselves, that we should not trust in ourselves, but in God who raises the dead,

Kikuyu: Nĩwe watũhonokirie ũgwati-inĩ ũcio wahaanaga wa gĩkuũ, na nowe ũgaatũhonokia. We nĩwe twĩrĩgĩrĩire atĩ no egũthiĩ na mbere gũtũhonokia,
English: who delivered us out of so great a death, and does deliver, on whom we have set our hope that he will also still deliver us,

Kikuyu: inyuĩ na inyuĩ mũgĩtũteithagia na gũtũhoera. Nao andũ aingĩ nĩmarĩcookagia ngaatho nĩ ũndũ witũ, tondũ wa wega ũcio tũtugĩĩtwo naguo arĩ macookio ma mahooya ma andũ aingĩ.
English: you also helping together on our behalf by your supplication; that, for the gift given to us by means of many, thanks may be given by many persons on your§ behalf.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ũndũ ũyũ nĩguo twĩrahagĩra: Thamiri ciitũ nĩcio itũmenyithagia atĩ mĩthiĩre iitũ ĩrĩa tũikaraga nayo gũkũ thĩ, na makĩria ũrĩa tũikaranagia na inyuĩ, nĩ ya ũtheru na ya ũhoro wa ma kuuma kũrĩ Ngai. Tũtiĩkaga ũguo kũringana na ũũgĩ wa thĩ, no nĩ kũringana na wega wa Ngai.
English: For our boasting is this: the testimony of our conscience that in holiness and sincerity of God, not in fleshly wisdom but in the grace of God, we behaved ourselves in the world, and more abundantly toward you.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo tũtiramwandĩkĩra ũndũ o na ũrĩkũ mũtangĩhota gũthoma kana gũtaũkĩrwo nĩguo. Na ngwĩhoka atĩ,
English: For we write no other things to you than what you read or even acknowledge, and I hope you will acknowledge to the end—

Kikuyu: o ta ũrĩa mũtũmenyete hanini, nĩmũrĩtaũkĩrwo biũ atĩ no mũhote kwĩraha nĩ ũndũ witũ, o ta ũrĩa tũkeeraha nĩ ũndũ wanyu mũthenya ũrĩa wa Mwathani Jesũ.
English: as also you acknowledged us in part—that we are your boasting, even as you also are ours, in the day of our Lord Jesus.

Kikuyu: Na tondũ wa ũrĩa ndaarĩ na mwĩhoko wa ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, nĩndabangĩte njũke kwanyu mbere nĩguo mũgunĩke maita meerĩ.
English: In this confidence, I was determined to come first to you, that you might have a second benefit,

Kikuyu: Ndabangĩte ndĩmũceerere ngĩthiĩ Makedonia, na njokere kũu kwanyu ngiuma Makedonia, na inyuĩ nĩguo mũcooke mũndũme Judea.
English: and by you to pass into Macedonia, and again from Macedonia to come to you, and to be sent forward by you on my journey to Judea.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndaabangaga ũndũ ũcio- rĩ, kaĩ ndeekaga ũguo na itherũ? Kana hihi haaragĩria mĩbango yakwa na njĩra ya kĩ-mwĩrĩ nĩguo rĩmwe njugage, “Ĩĩ, ĩĩ,” na rĩrĩa rĩngĩ ngoiga “Aca, aca”?
English: When I therefore planned this, did I show fickleness? Or the things that I plan, do I plan according to the flesh, that with me there should be the “Yes, yes” and the “No, no?”

Kikuyu: No ti-itherũ, o ta ũrĩa Ngai arĩ mwĩhokeku-rĩ, ndũmĩrĩri iitũ kũrĩ inyuĩ ti “Ĩĩ” na rĩngĩ “Aca.”
English: But as God is faithful, our word toward you was not “Yes and no.”

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Mũrũ wa Ngai, Jesũ Kristũ, ũrĩa mwahunjĩirio ũhoro wake nĩ niĩ, na Sila, na Timotheo, ũhoro wake ndwarĩ wa kuuga, “Ĩĩ” ũgacooka ũkoiga “Aca,” no thĩinĩ wake gũtũũraga o “Ĩĩ.”
English: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by us—by me, Silvanus, and Timothy—was not “Yes and no,” but in him is “Yes.”

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ciĩranĩro cia Ngai, o na cingĩhĩte atĩa-rĩ, ciothe nĩ “Ĩĩ” thĩinĩ wa Kristũ. Na nĩ ũndũ ũcio thĩinĩ wake, ithuĩ tũkoigaga “Ameni” nĩgeetha Ngai agoocagwo.
English: For however many are the promises of God, in him is the “Yes.” Therefore also through him is the “Amen”, to the glory of God through us.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Ngai nĩwe ũtũmaga ithuĩ hamwe na inyuĩ twĩhaande wega thĩinĩ wa Kristũ. Nĩwe watũitĩrĩirie maguta,
English: Now he who establishes us with you in Christ and anointed us is God,

Kikuyu: na agĩtwĩkĩra rũũri rwa kuonania atĩ tũrĩ ake, na agĩtũhe Roho wake thĩinĩ wa ngoro ciitũ arĩ ta mwĩhoko wa kũrũgamĩrĩra maũndũ marĩa magooka.
English: who also sealed us and gave us the down payment of the Spirit in our hearts.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩwe mũira wakwa, na nĩoĩ atĩ niĩ ndaagire gũcooka Korinitho nĩ ũndũ wa kũmũcaaĩra.
English: But I call God for a witness to my soul, that to spare you, I didn’t come to Corinth.

Kikuyu: Ũguo ti kuuga atĩ nĩtũramwatha ũhoro-inĩ wa wĩtĩkio wanyu, no nĩ atĩ tũrutithanagia wĩra na inyuĩ nĩgeetha mũkenage, tondũ mũtũũrĩte mwĩhaandĩte wega nĩ ũndũ wa wĩtĩkio wanyu.
English: We don’t control your faith, but are fellow workers with you for your joy. For you stand firm in faith.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩgũtua ndaatuire itua atĩ ndikũmũceerera rĩngĩ ndĩmũiguithie kĩeha.
English: But I determined this for myself, that I would not come to you again in sorrow.

Kikuyu: Tondũ- rĩ, ingĩkĩmũiguithia kĩeha-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩgĩtigara wa kũngenia, o tiga inyuĩ arĩa njiguithĩtie kĩeha?
English: For if I make you grieve, then who will make me glad but he who is made to grieve by me?

Kikuyu: Ndamwandĩkĩire ũguo nĩgeetha rĩrĩa ngooka ndikanaiguithio kĩeha nĩ acio njagĩrĩirwo nĩgũkenio nĩo. Nĩ ndaarĩ na ũũmĩrĩru harĩ inyuĩ inyuothe atĩ nĩmũngĩagwatanĩire na niĩ gĩkeno-inĩ gĩakwa, nakĩo gĩtuĩke kĩanyu.
English: And I wrote this very thing to you, so that when I came, I wouldn’t have sorrow from them of whom I ought to rejoice; having confidence in you all that my joy would be shared by all of you.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ndamwandĩkĩire ndĩ mũthĩĩnĩku mũno, na ndĩ na ruo rwa ngoro na maithori maingĩ, ti atĩ nĩguo ndĩmũiguithie kĩeha, no nĩguo ndĩmũmenyithie wendo ũrĩa ndĩmwendete naguo.
English: For out of much affliction and anguish of heart I wrote to you with many tears, not that you should be made to grieve, but that you might know the love that I have so abundantly for you.

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo nĩ kũrĩ mũndũ ũiguithĩtie amwe anyu kĩeha-rĩ, ti niĩ aiguithĩtie, no nĩ inyuothe aiguithĩtie kĩeha. Ndiroiga ũguo ngĩhatĩrĩria ũrĩa wĩkĩte ũguo.
English: But if any has caused sorrow, he has caused sorrow not to me, but in part (that I not press too heavily) to you all.

Kikuyu: Iherithia rĩrĩa aherithĩtio nĩ andũ arĩa aingĩ anyu, nĩrĩmũiganĩte.
English: This punishment which was inflicted by the many is sufficient for such a one;

Kikuyu: Rĩu inyuĩ-rĩ, nĩmwagĩrĩirwo nĩkũmũrekera na kũmũũmĩrĩria, nĩguo ndakae gũtoorio nĩ kĩeha gĩkĩrĩte.
English: so that, on the contrary, you should rather forgive him and comfort him, lest by any means such a one should be swallowed up with his excessive sorrow.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ndamũthaitha mũmuonie atĩ no mũmwendete.
English: Therefore I beg you to confirm your love toward him.

Kikuyu: Gĩtũmi kĩrĩa gĩatũmire ndĩmwandĩkĩre marũa macio nĩndĩmũgerie, na menye kana no mwĩtiirie magerio na mwathĩkage maũndũ-inĩ mothe.
English: For to this end I also wrote, that I might know the proof of you, whether you are obedient in all things.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mũngĩrekera, o na niĩ nĩndamũrekera. Naguo ũndũ ũrĩa ndekanĩire-rĩ (hangĩkorwo nĩ haarĩ ũndũ o na ũrĩkũ wa kũrekanĩra), ũndũ ũcio ndaũrekanĩra nĩ ũndũ wanyu ndĩ mbere ya Kristũ,
English: Now I also forgive whomever you forgive anything. For if indeed I have forgiven anything, I have forgiven that one for your sakes in the presence of Christ,

Kikuyu: nĩguo Shaitani ndakae gũtũhoota. Nĩgũkorwo tũtirigĩtwo nĩ mawara make.
English: that no advantage may be gained over us by Satan, for we are not ignorant of his schemes.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, rĩrĩa ndaathiire Teroa kũhunjia Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega wa Kristũ, na ngĩkora Mwathani ahingũrĩte mũrango-rĩ,
English: Now when I came to Troas for the Good News of Christ, and when a door was opened to me in the Lord,

Kikuyu: ndiigana kũgĩa na thayũ ngoro-inĩ yakwa, tondũ ndiakorire mũrũ-wa-Ithe witũ Tito kuo. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngiugĩra andũ akuo ũhoro, ngĩcooka ngĩthiĩ nginya Makedonia.
English: I had no relief for my spirit, because I didn’t find Titus my brother, but taking my leave of them, I went out into Macedonia.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Ngai arogoocwo, o we ũrĩa ũtũtongoragia hĩndĩ ciothe na ũhootani thĩinĩ wa Kristũ, nake agatũhũthagĩra ta tũrĩ mũtararĩko mwega wa kũmemerekia ũhoro wa kũmenya Kristũ kũndũ guothe.
English: Now thanks be to God who always leads us in triumph in Christ, and reveals through us the sweet aroma of his knowledge in every place.

Kikuyu: Tondũ ithuĩ tũrĩ mũtararĩko wa Kristũ ũrĩa ũkinyaga harĩ Ngai, kũrĩ arĩa marahonokio o na arĩa maroora.
English: For we are a sweet aroma of Christ to God in those who are being saved and in those who perish:

Kikuyu: Kũrĩ arĩa maroora-rĩ, tũrĩ mũnungo mũũru wa gĩkuũ; no kũrĩ arĩa marahonokio-rĩ, tũrĩ mũtararĩko wa muoyo. Nũũ ũngĩhota wĩra ta ũcio?
English: to the one a stench from death to death, to the other a sweet aroma from life to life. Who is sufficient for these things?

Kikuyu: Ithuĩ tũtiĩkaga ta ũrĩa andũ aingĩ mekaga; tũtionjorithagia kiugo kĩa Ngai nĩguo tuone uumithio. Handũ ha gwĩka ũguo-rĩ, ithuĩ twaragia tũrĩ na wĩhokeku thĩinĩ wa Kristũ mbere ya Ngai, ta andũ matũmĩtwo nĩ Ngai.
English: For we are not as so many, peddling the word of God. But as of sincerity, but as of God, in the sight of God, we speak in Christ.

Kikuyu: Anga nĩtũraambĩrĩria gũcooka kwĩgaathĩrĩria? Kana hihi nĩtũbataire kũheo marũa ma gũtũ- gaathĩrĩria kũrĩ inyuĩ kana moimĩte kũrĩ inyuĩ ta andũ arĩa angĩ?
English: Are we beginning again to commend ourselves? Or do we need, as do some, letters of commendation to you or from you?

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ ene nĩ inyuĩ marũa maitũ, maandĩkĩtwo ngoro-inĩ ciitũ, mamenyekete na magathomagwo nĩ andũ othe.
English: You are our letter, written in our hearts, known and read by all men,

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ nĩmuonanagia atĩ mũrĩ marũa moimĩte kũrĩ Kristũ, inyuĩ mũrĩ maciaro ma ũtungata witũ, marũa mataandĩkĩtwo na rangi, no maandĩkĩtwo na Roho wa Ngai ũrĩa ũrĩ muoyo. Matiandĩkĩtwo ihengere-inĩ cia mahiga, no maandĩkĩtwo ihengere-inĩ cia ngoro cia andũ.
English: being revealed that you are a letter of Christ, served by us, written not with ink, but with the Spirit of the living God; not in tablets of stone, but in tablets that are hearts of flesh.

Kikuyu: Ũcio nĩguo ũũmĩrĩru witũ harĩ Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa Kristũ.
English: Such confidence we have through Christ toward God,

Kikuyu: Na ti atĩ tũrĩ na ũhoti ithuĩ ene tũngĩĩgaathĩra, no ũhoti witũ uumaga kũrĩ Ngai.
English: not that we are sufficient of ourselves to account anything as from ourselves; but our sufficiency is from God,

Kikuyu: Nĩwe ũtũhotithĩtie tũtuĩke ndungata cia kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa kĩerũ, nakĩo ti kĩa ũhoro ũrĩa mwandĩke, no nĩ kĩa Roho; nĩgũkorwo ũhoro ũrĩa mwandĩke nĩ kũũraga ũragaga, no Roho aheanaga muoyo.
English: who also made us sufficient as servants of a new covenant, not of the letter but of the Spirit. For the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, angĩkorwo ũtungata ũrĩa wa tene, ũrĩa wandĩkĩtwo na ndemwa ihengere-inĩ cia mahiga, wokire ũrĩ na riiri, o nginya andũ a Isiraeli makĩremwo nĩgũikara marorete ũthiũ wa Musa tondũ wa ũrĩa warĩ na riiri, o na gũtuĩka riiri ũcio nĩwathiraga-rĩ,
English: But if the service of death, written engraved on stones, came with glory, so that the children of Israel could not look steadfastly on the face of Moses for the glory of his face, which was passing away,

Kikuyu: githĩ ũtungata wa Roho to ũgĩkorwo ũkĩrĩ na riiri makĩria?
English: won’t service of the Spirit be with much more glory?

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo ũtungata ũrĩa ũtuaga andũ ehia nĩ ũrĩ riiri-rĩ, githĩ ũtungata ũrĩa ũrehaga ũthingu ndũngĩgĩkorwo ũrĩ na riiri makĩria!
English: For if the service of condemnation has glory, the service of righteousness exceeds much more in glory.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũhoro ũrĩa warĩ na riiri mbere, rĩu ndũrĩ na riiri ũkĩringithanio na riiri ũcio ũngĩ ũũkĩrĩte.
English: For most certainly that which has been made glorious has not been made glorious in this respect, by reason of the glory that surpasses.

Kikuyu: Na angĩkorwo ũhoro ũcio wathiraga wokire na riiri-rĩ, ũcio ũngĩ wa gũtũũra ndũngĩgĩkorwo na riiri makĩria!
English: For if that which passes away was with glory, much more that which remains is in glory.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, twaragia ũhoro tũũmĩrĩirie mũno, nĩ ũndũ wa kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro kĩu.
English: Having therefore such a hope, we use great boldness of speech,

Kikuyu: Ithuĩ tũtitariĩ ta Musa, ũrĩa wehumbagĩra ũthiũ na gĩtambaya nĩguo agirĩrĩrie andũ a Isiraeli gũcũthĩrĩria ũthiũ wake rĩrĩa ũkengi ũcio wathiraga.
English: and not as Moses, who put a veil on his face so that the children of Israel wouldn’t look steadfastly on the end of that which was passing away.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, meciiria mao nĩmaritirio, nĩgũkorwo nginya ũmũthĩ gĩtambaya o kĩu gĩkoragwo o ho hĩndĩ ĩrĩa kĩrĩkanĩro kĩu gĩkũrũ kĩao gĩgũthomwo. Gĩtambaya kĩu gĩtirĩ kĩeherio, tondũ kĩeheragio nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia Kristũ.
English: But their minds were hardened, for until this very day at the reading of the old covenant the same veil remains, because in Christ it passes away.

Kikuyu: O na nginya ũmũthĩ rĩrĩa watho wa Musa ũgũthomwo- rĩ, gĩtambaya kĩu no kĩhumbagĩra ngoro ciao.
English: But to this day, when Moses is read, a veil lies on their heart.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa rĩothe mũndũ o na ũ agarũrũkĩra Mwathani- rĩ, gĩtambaya kĩu nĩkĩeheragio.
English: But whenever someone turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Mwathani nĩwe Roho, na harĩa Roho wa Mwathani arĩ, hau nĩ harĩ na wĩyathi.
English: Now the Lord is the Spirit; and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty.

Kikuyu: Na ithuĩ ithuothe arĩa tũtahumbĩrĩte mothiũ maitũ, nĩtũhenahenagia riiri wa Mwathani ta tũrĩ icicio, na nĩtũgarũragwo nĩguo tũhaanane nake tũrĩ na riiri wa gũtũũra ũrĩa ũthiiaga o ũkĩongererekaga, o uumĩte kũrĩ Mwathani, ũrĩa arĩ we Roho.
English: But we all, with unveiled face seeing the glory of the Lord as in a mirror, are transformed into the same image from glory to glory, even as from the Lord, the Spirit.

Kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio, kuona atĩ nĩ tũrĩ na ũtungata ũcio nĩ ũndũ wa tha cia Ngai-rĩ, tũtiũragwo nĩ hinya.
English: Therefore, seeing we have this ministry, even as we obtained mercy, we don’t faint.

Kikuyu: No ithuĩ nĩtũreganĩte na mĩthiĩre ya hitho na ya thoni; ithuĩ tũtithiiaga na mĩthiĩre ya wara na tũtitukanagia kiugo kĩa Ngai na maheeni. Handũ ha ũguo-rĩ, nĩ ũhoro wa ma tũheanaga hatarĩ ũndũ tũkuongerera, na tũrĩ mbere ya Ngai, nĩtũgeragia gũtũma thamiri cia o mũndũ itwĩtĩkĩre.
English: But we have renounced the hidden things of shame, not walking in craftiness nor handling the word of God deceitfully, but by the manifestation of the truth commending ourselves to every man’s conscience in the sight of God.

Kikuyu: O na angĩkorwo ũhoro witũ ũrĩa tũhunjagia nĩmũhumbĩre-rĩ, ũhumbĩrĩtwo kũrĩ arĩa maroora.
English: Even if our Good News is veiled, it is veiled in those who are dying,

Kikuyu: Nayo ngai ya mahinda maya, nĩtũmĩte meciiria ma andũ arĩa matetĩkĩtie matumane, nĩgeetha matigathererwo nĩ ũtheri wa Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega wa riiri wa Kristũ, o we ũrĩa arĩ we mũhaanĩre wa Ngai.
English: in whom the god of this world has blinded the minds of the unbelieving, that the light of the Good News of the glory of Christ, who is the image of God, should not dawn on them.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ti ũhoro witũ ithuĩ ene tũhunjagia, no tũhunjagia Jesũ Kristũ arĩ we Mwathani, na ithuĩ tũrĩ ndungata cianyu nĩ ũndũ wa Jesũ.
English: For we don’t preach ourselves, but Christ Jesus as Lord, and ourselves as your servants for Jesus’ sake,

Kikuyu: Tondũ Ngai ũrĩa woigire, “Ũtheri nĩware nduma-inĩ,” nĩwe watũmire ũtheri wake ware ngoro-inĩ ciitũ nĩguo tũheo ũtheri wa kũmenyithagia andũ riiri wa Ngai ũcio wonekaga ũthiũ-inĩ wa Kristũ.
English: seeing it is God who said, “Light will shine out of darkness,”* who has shone in our hearts to give the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, ithuĩ tũkuuĩte kĩgĩĩna kĩu tũhaana ta tũrĩ na nyũngũ cia rĩũmba* nĩgeetha kũmenyeke atĩ ũhoti ũyũ mũnene uumĩte kũrĩ Ngai no nduumaga kũrĩ ithuĩ.
English: But we have this treasure in clay vessels, that the exceeding greatness of the power may be of God and not from ourselves.

Kikuyu: Ithuĩ tũhatĩkĩtwo mũno na mĩena yothe, no tũtihehenjetwo; nĩtũrigĩtwo nĩ wa gwĩka, no tũtikuĩte ngoro;
English: We are pressed on every side, yet not crushed; perplexed, yet not to despair;

Kikuyu: na nĩtũnyariirĩtwo, no tũtitiganĩirio; nĩtũrũndĩtwo, no tũtiniinĩtwo.
English: pursued, yet not forsaken; struck down, yet not destroyed;

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ciothe tũkoragwo tũkuuĩte gĩkuũ kĩa Jesũ na mĩĩrĩ iitũ, nĩguo muoyo wa Jesũ o naguo ũguũranagĩrio thĩinĩ wa mĩĩrĩ iitũ.
English: always carrying in the body the putting to death of the Lord Jesus, that the life of Jesus may also be revealed in our body.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ithuĩ arĩa tũrĩ muoyo tũtũire tũneanagwo gĩkuũ-inĩ nĩ ũndũ wa Jesũ, nĩgeetha muoyo wake ũguũranagĩrio thĩinĩ wa mĩĩrĩ ĩno iitũ ĩkuuaga.
English: For we who live are always delivered to death for Jesus’ sake, that the life also of Jesus may be revealed in our mortal flesh.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo-rĩ, gĩkuũ kĩrutaga wĩra thĩinĩ witũ, no muoyo ũkaruta wĩra thĩinĩ wanyu.
English: So then death works in us, but life in you.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Tondũ nĩndetĩkirie; nĩkĩo ndaaririe.” Nĩ ũndũ wa roho o ro ũcio wa wĩtĩkio-rĩ, o na ithuĩ nĩtwĩtĩkĩtie na nĩ ũndũũcio tũkaaria,
English: But having the same spirit of faith, according to that which is written, “I believed, and therefore I spoke.”* We also believe, and therefore we also speak,

Kikuyu: tondũ nĩtũũĩ atĩ ũrĩa wariũkirieMwathani Jesũ kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ, o na ithuĩ nĩagatũriũkia hamwe na Jesũ, na atũrũgamie mbere yake hamwe na inyuĩ.
English: knowing that he who raised the Lord Jesus will raise us also through† Jesus, and will present us with you.

Kikuyu: Maũndũ maya mothe nĩmakũmũguna, nĩgeetha wega ũrĩa ũrakinyĩrĩra andũ aingĩ makĩria ũtũme ngaatho ciyũrĩrĩre nĩgeetha Ngai agoocagwo.
English: For all things are for your sakes, that the grace, being multiplied through the many, may cause the thanksgiving to abound to the glory of God.

Kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio tũtiũragwo nĩ hinya. O na gũtuĩka mwena wa nja nĩtũrathirĩrĩkĩra, no mũndũ wa thĩinĩ nĩ kwerũhio erũhagio o mũthenya.
English: Therefore we don’t faint, but though our outward person is decaying, yet our inward person is renewed day by day.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mathĩĩna maitũ mahũthũ na ma ihinda inini maratũma tũreherwo riiri ũmakĩrĩte wa tene na tene.
English: For our light affliction, which is for the moment, works for us more and more exceedingly an eternal weight of glory,

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, tũticũthagĩrĩria kĩrĩa kĩroneka, no tũcũthagĩrĩria kĩrĩa gĩtonekaga. Nĩgũkorwo kĩrĩa kĩonekaga nĩ gĩa kahinda, no kĩrĩa gĩtonekaga nĩ gĩa gũtũũra tene na tene. 5
English: while we don’t look at the things which are seen, but at the things which are not seen. For the things which are seen are temporal, but the things which are not seen are eternal.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, nĩtũũĩ atĩ hema ĩno ya gũkũ thĩ, ĩno tũtũũraga thĩinĩ wayo, ĩngĩtharũrio-rĩ, nĩ tũrĩ na nyũmba ĩngĩ yumĩte kũrĩ Ngai, nyũmba ĩtaakĩtwo na moko ma andũ, ya gũtũũra tene na tene ĩrĩ kũu igũrũ.
English: For we know that if the earthly house of our tent is dissolved, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal, in the heavens.

Kikuyu: Rĩu nĩtũcaayaga, tũkĩĩriragĩria kũhumbwo na nyũmba ĩyo ĩngĩ iitũ ya kuuma igũrũ,
English: For most certainly in this we groan, longing to be clothed with our habitation which is from heaven,

Kikuyu: tondũ twahumbwo ũguo tũtigakorwo tũrĩ njaga.
English: if indeed being clothed, we will not be found naked.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo rĩrĩa rĩothe tũrĩ thĩinĩ wa hema ĩno-rĩ, nĩtũcaayaga na tũkaritũhĩrwo, tondũ tũtiendaga kwaũrwo, no twendaga tũhumbwo na mwĩrĩ ũcio witũ wa kuuma igũrũ, nĩgeetha mwĩrĩ ũyũ wa gũkua ũmerio nĩ muoyo.
English: For indeed we who are in this tent do groan, being burdened, not that we desire to be unclothed, but that we desire to be clothed, that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Ngai nĩwe ũtũmbĩte nĩ ũndũ wa gĩtũmi kĩu kĩũmbe, na nĩatũheete Roho arĩ ta mwĩhoko wa kũrũgamĩrĩra maũndũ marĩa magooka.
English: Now he who made us for this very thing is God, who also gave to us the down payment of the Spirit.

Kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio tũikaraga hĩndĩ ciothe tũrĩ na ũũmĩrĩru, tũkĩmenyaga atĩ rĩrĩa rĩothe tũikaraga thĩinĩ wa mwĩrĩ taarĩ guo mũciĩ witũ, tũikaraga tũraihanĩrĩirie na Mwathani.
English: Therefore we are always confident and know that while we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord;

Kikuyu: Tũtũũragio nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia, no tũtitũũragio nĩ ũndũ wa kuona na maitho.
English: for we walk by faith, not by sight.

Kikuyu: Nguuga atĩrĩ, nĩ tũrĩ na ũũmĩrĩru atĩ ũndũ ũrĩa tũngĩenda nĩ tũkorwo tũtarĩ mwĩrĩ-inĩ ũyũ, no tũkorwo tũrĩ mũciĩ hamwe na Mwathani.
English: We are courageous, I say, and are willing rather to be absent from the body and to be at home with the Lord.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, nĩtũgeragia na kĩyo muoroto witũ ũtuĩke wa kũmũkenia, twakorwo tũrĩ mwĩrĩ-inĩ ũyũ witũ o na kana twakorwo tuumĩte thĩinĩ waguo.
English: Therefore also we make it our aim, whether at home or absent, to be well pleasing to him.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo no nginya ithuothe tũkaarũgama mbere ya gĩtĩ gĩa ciira gĩa Kristũ, nĩguo o mũndũ akaaheo kĩrĩa kĩmwagĩrĩire kũringana na maũndũ marĩa aaneka arĩ thĩinĩ wa mwĩrĩ, marĩ mega kana marĩ mooru.
English: For we must all be revealed before the judgment seat of Christ that each one may receive the things in the body according to what he has done, whether good or bad.

Kikuyu: Kuona atĩ nĩtũmenyete ũrĩa Mwathani aagĩrĩirwo nĩ gwĩtigĩrwo-rĩ, nĩkĩo tũgeragia kũringĩrĩria andũ. Ũrĩa tũtariĩ nĩ ũndũ ũĩkaine wega nĩ Ngai, na nĩngwĩhoka atĩ o na nĩũĩkaine wega ngoro-inĩ cianyu.
English: Knowing therefore the fear of the Lord, we persuade men, but we are revealed to God, and I hope that we are revealed also in your consciences.

Kikuyu: Tikũgeria tũrageria kwĩgaathĩrĩria harĩ inyuĩ rĩngĩ, no nĩ mweke tũramũhe wa kwĩraha nĩ ũndũ witũ, nĩguo mũhotage gũcookeria arĩa merahaga na maũndũ marĩa monagwo na maitho gũkĩra marĩa marĩ ngoro-inĩ.
English: For we are not commending ourselves to you again, but speak as giving you occasion of boasting on our behalf, that you may have something to answer those who boast in appearance and not in heart.

Kikuyu: Tũngĩkorwo nĩkũgũrũka tũgũrũkĩte-rĩ, tũgũrũkĩte nĩ ũndũ wa Ngai; na tũngĩkorwo meciiria maitũ nĩ mega-rĩ, tũhaana ũguo nĩ ũndũ wanyu.
English: For if we are beside ourselves, it is for God. Or if we are of sober mind, it is for you.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo wendo wa Kristũ nĩguo ũtũringagĩrĩria, tondũ nĩtũmenyete wega atĩ mũndũ ũmwe nĩwe wakuire nĩ ũndũ wa andũ othe, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, andũ othe nĩmakuire.
English: For the love of Christ compels us; because we judge thus: that one died for all, therefore all died.

Kikuyu: Nake aakuire nĩ ũndũ wa andũ othe, nĩgeetha arĩa matũũraga muoyo matigacooke gũtũũra muoyo nĩ ũndũ wao ene, no matũũre nĩ ũndũ wa ũcio wakuire nĩ ũndũ wao na agĩcooka akĩriũka.
English: He died for all, that those who live should no longer live to themselves, but to him who for their sakes died and rose again.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, kuuma rĩu tũtigũcooka gũtua ũhoro wa mũndũ na njĩra ya kĩ-mwĩrĩ. O na gwatuĩka kũrĩ hĩndĩ tuonaga Kristũ na njĩra ĩyo-rĩ, rĩu tũtiĩkaga ũguo.
English: Therefore we know no one according to the flesh from now on. Even though we have known Christ according to the flesh, yet now we know him so no more.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, mũndũ o wothe angĩkorwo arĩ thĩinĩ wa Kristũ-rĩ, nĩatuĩkĩte kĩũmbe kĩerũ; maũndũ ma tene nĩmathirĩte, nĩmookĩte mangĩ merũ!
English: Therefore if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old things have passed away. Behold,† all things have become new.

Kikuyu: Maũndũ maya mothe moimaga kũrĩ Ngai, ũrĩa watũiguithanirie nake we mwene thĩinĩ wa Kristũ, na agĩtũhe ũtungata ũcio wa ũiguithanio:
English: But all things are of God, who reconciled us to himself through Jesus Christ, and gave to us the ministry of reconciliation;

Kikuyu: atĩrĩ, Ngai arĩ thĩinĩ wa Kristũ nĩwe wĩĩiguithanagĩria thĩ kũrĩ we mwene, ategũtarĩra andũ mehia mao. Nake nĩatwĩhokeire ndũmĩrĩri ĩyo ya ũiguithanio.
English: namely, that God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not reckoning to them their trespasses, and having committed to us the word of reconciliation.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ithuĩ tũrĩ abarũthi matũmĩtwo a kwarĩrĩria Kristũ, o taarĩ Ngai ũrathaithana na tũnua twitũ. Twamũthaitha handũ-inĩ ha Kristũ atĩrĩ: Ĩtĩkĩrai kũiguithanio na Ngai.
English: We are therefore ambassadors on behalf of Christ, as though God were entreating by us: we beg you on behalf of Christ, be reconciled to God.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩatũmire ũcio ũtaarĩ na wĩhia atuĩke mwĩhia nĩ ũndũ witũ, nĩgeetha thĩinĩ wake tũtuĩke ũthingu wa Ngai.
English: For him who knew no sin he made to be sin on our behalf, so that in him we might become the righteousness of God.

Kikuyu: Tondũ tũrĩ aruti a wĩra hamwe na Ngai-rĩ, nĩtũkũmũringĩrĩria mũtikae kwamũkĩra wega wa Ngai o ũguo tũhũ.
English: Working together, we entreat also that you do not receive the grace of God in vain.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Hĩndĩ ya gwĩtĩkĩrĩka-rĩ, nĩndagũthikĩrĩirie, naguo mũthenya wa kũhonokanio-rĩ, nĩndagũteithirie.” Atĩrĩrĩ-i, rĩĩrĩ nĩrĩo ihinda rĩa gwĩtĩkĩrĩka nĩ Ngai, naguo ũyũ nĩguo mũthenya wa kũhonokanio.
English: For he says, “At an acceptable time I listened to you. In a day of salvation I helped you.”* Behold, now is the acceptable time. Behold, now is the day of salvation.

Kikuyu: Ithuĩ-rĩ, tũtitũmaga hagĩe na ũndũ ũngĩhĩnga mũndũ nĩguo ũtungata witũ ndũkonerwo mahĩtia.
English: We give no occasion of stumbling in anything, that our service may not be blamed,

Kikuyu: Aca, ithuĩ tũrĩ ta ndungata cia Ngai nĩtwĩgathagĩrĩria maũndũ-inĩ mothe: nĩ ũndũ wa kũũmĩrĩria maũndũ maingĩ; na gũkirĩrĩria mathĩĩna na moritũ, na mĩtangĩko;
English: but in everything commending ourselves as servants of God: in great endurance, in afflictions, in hardships, in distresses,

Kikuyu: na ũndũ wa kũhũũrwo, na kuohwo njeera, na kũreherwo ngũĩ; na kũruta wĩra mũritũ, na kũũrwo nĩ toro ũtukũ, na ngʼaragu;
English: in beatings, in imprisonments, in riots, in labors, in watchings, in fastings,

Kikuyu: ningĩ nĩ ũndũ wa gũtũũra na ũthingu, na ũtaũku, na gũkirĩrĩria, na gũtugana thĩinĩ wa Roho Mũtheru, na kũgĩa na wendani ũtarĩ na ũhinga;
English: in pureness, in knowledge, in perseverance, in kindness, in the Holy Spirit, in sincere love,

Kikuyu: o na kwaria ũhoro wa ma, na gũkorwo tũrĩ na hinya wa Ngai; nĩgũkorwo tũnyiitĩte indo cia mbaara cia ũthingu guoko-inĩ kwa ũrĩo na kwa ũmotho;
English: in the word of truth, in the power of God, by the armor of righteousness on the right hand and on the left,

Kikuyu: rĩmwe tũheagwo gĩtĩĩo, na rĩrĩa rĩngĩ tũgaconorwo, rĩmwe tũgacuukĩrwo maũndũ mooru na rĩngĩ mega; ningĩ o na tũrĩ andũ a ma, tũgatuuo a maheeni;
English: by glory and dishonor, by evil report and good report, as deceivers and yet true,

Kikuyu: rĩmwe tũgatuuo nĩtũũĩkaine, no rĩrĩa rĩngĩ tũgatuuo ta tũtoĩkaine; ningĩ tũkahaana ta tũraakua, no tũrĩ o muoyo; tũhũũragwo, no tũtiũragagwo;
English: as unknown and yet well known, as dying and behold—we live, as punished and not killed,

Kikuyu: o na tũngĩonwo ta tũrĩ na kĩeha, ithuĩ tũkoragwo tũkenete hĩndĩ ciothe; tuonekaga tũrĩ athĩĩni, no nĩtũtuaga andũ aingĩ itonga; tũtuagwo ta tũtarĩ kĩndũ, no indo ciothe nĩ ciitũ.
English: as sorrowful yet always rejoicing, as poor yet making many rich, as having nothing and yet possessing all things.

Kikuyu: Akorinitho aya, tũramwarĩria hatarĩ ũndũ tũkũmũhitha, na tũkamũhingũrĩra ngoro ciitũ biũ.
English: Our mouth is open to you, Corinthians. Our heart is enlarged.

Kikuyu: Ithuĩ nĩtũmuonetie wendo witũ kũna no inyuĩ mũtiratuonia wendo wanyu.
English: You are not restricted by us, but you are restricted by your own affections.

Kikuyu: O ta ũguo tũmwĩkĩte- rĩ, ndĩramwarĩria ta ciana ciakwa, o na inyuĩ hingũrai ngoro cianyu biũ.
English: Now in return—I speak as to my children—you also open your hearts.

Kikuyu: Mũtikae kuohanio na arĩa matetĩkĩtie ta mwĩkĩrĩtwo icooki rĩmwe nao. Tondũ-rĩ, ũthingu na waganu ingĩgwatanĩra atĩa? Kana ũtheri na nduma-rĩ, ingĩgĩa na ngwatanĩro ĩrĩkũ?
English: Don’t be unequally yoked with unbelievers, for what fellowship do righteousness and iniquity have? Or what fellowship does light have with darkness?

Kikuyu: Kristũ na Beliali-rĩ, marĩ na ũiguano ũrĩkũ? Nake mũndũ ũrĩa wĩtĩkĩtie na ũrĩa ũtetĩkĩtie-rĩ, marĩ na ngwatanĩro ĩrĩkũ?
English: What agreement does Christ have with Belial? Or what portion does a believer have with an unbeliever?

Kikuyu: Hekarũ ya Ngai yakĩhota atĩa kũiguana na mĩhianano? Nĩgũkorwo ithuĩ nĩ ithuĩ hekarũ ya Ngai ũrĩa ũrĩ muoyo. O ta ũrĩa Ngai oigĩte atĩrĩ: “Nĩndĩrĩtũũranagia nao, na njeerage gatagatĩ-inĩ kao, nduĩke Ngai wao, nao matuĩke andũ akwa.”
English: What agreement does a temple of God have with idols? For you are a temple of the living God. Even as God said, “I will dwell in them and walk in them. I will be their God and they will be my people.”*

Kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, umai kũrĩ o na mwĩyamũranie nao, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani ekuuga. Mũtikahutie kĩndũ kĩrĩ thaahu, na niĩ nĩngũmwamũkĩra.”
English: Therefore “ ‘Come out from among them, and be separate,’ says the Lord. ‘Touch no unclean thing. I will receive you.*

Kikuyu: “Nĩngũtuĩka Ithe wanyu, na inyuĩ mũtuĩke ariũ akwa na aarĩ akwa, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani-Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga.”
English: I will be to you a Father. You will be to me sons and daughters,’ says the Lord Almighty.”*

Kikuyu: Arata akwa endwa, kuona atĩ nĩtwĩrĩirwo maũndũ macio-rĩ, nĩtwĩtheriei, tũtigane na ũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ũthũkagia mũndũ mwĩrĩ na roho, na tũhingagie ũhoro wa ũtheru nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtigĩra Ngai.
English: Having therefore these promises, beloved, let’s cleanse ourselves from all defilement of flesh and spirit, perfecting holiness in the fear of God.

Kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, tũhei handũ ngoro-inĩ cianyu. Tũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ tũhĩtĩirie, na tũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ tũthũkĩtie, na gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ tũhinyĩrĩirie.
English: Open your hearts to us. We wronged no one. We corrupted no one. We took advantage of no one.

Kikuyu: Ndiroiga ũguo nĩguo ndĩmũtuĩre; nĩnjugĩte rĩngĩ atĩ mũrĩ na handũ thĩinĩ wa ngoro ciitũ, kuona atĩ tũgũtũũra muoyo kana tũkuanĩre na inyuĩ.
English: I say this not to condemn you, for I have said before that you are in our hearts to die together and live together.

Kikuyu: Ndĩ na ũũmĩrĩru mũnene ngĩmwarĩria; nĩndĩĩrahaga mũno nĩ ũndũ wanyu. Mathĩĩna-inĩ maitũ mothe, nĩngoragwo nyũmĩrĩirie mũno, na gĩkeno gĩakwa gĩgakĩrĩrĩria.
English: Great is my boldness of speech toward you. Great is my boasting on your behalf. I am filled with comfort. I overflow with joy in all our affliction.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo rĩrĩa twakinyire Makedonia, mwĩrĩ ũyũ witũ ndwagĩire na kĩhurũko, no gũthĩĩnio twathĩĩnagio o harĩa hothe twathiiaga; kũu nja kwarĩ na ngũĩ, na ngoro-inĩ ciitũ twarĩ na guoya.
English: For even when we had come into Macedonia, our flesh had no relief, but we were afflicted on every side. Fightings were outside. Fear was inside.

Kikuyu: No Ngai ũrĩa ũhooragĩria arĩa makuĩte ngoro, nĩatũhooreririe nĩ ũndũ wa gũũka gwa Tito,
English: Nevertheless, he who comforts the lowly, God, comforted us by the coming of Titus,

Kikuyu: na to ũndũ wa gũũka gwake gwiki, no ningĩ nĩ ũndũ wa ũhoorerania ũrĩa mwamũheete. Nĩatũheire ũhoro wa ũrĩa mwendaga kũnyona, na kĩeha kĩnene kĩrĩa mũrĩ nakĩo, o na ũrĩa mwĩtangaga mũno nĩ ũndũ wakwa; nĩ ũndũ ũcio gĩkeno gĩakwa gĩkĩongerereka gũkĩra mbere.
English: and not by his coming only, but also by the comfort with which he was comforted in you while he told us of your longing, your mourning, and your zeal for me, so that I rejoiced still more.

Kikuyu: O na ingĩkorwo nĩndatũmire mũgĩe na kĩeha nĩ ũndũ wa marũa makwa-rĩ, ndikwĩrira. O na gũkorwo kũrĩ hĩndĩ nderirire nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, nguona atĩ marũa makwa nĩmamũiguithirie kĩeha, no nĩ gwa kahinda o kanini,
English: For though I grieved you with my letter, I do not regret it, though I did regret it. For I see that my letter made you grieve, though just for a while.

Kikuyu: no rĩu ndĩ na gĩkeno, ti tondũ nĩmwaiguithirio kĩeha, no nĩ tondũ kĩeha kĩanyu nĩgĩatũmire mũhere. Nĩgũkorwo mwagĩire na kĩeha o ta ũrĩa Ngai endaga, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio ithuĩ tũtirĩ ũndũ mũũru o na ũmwe twamwĩkire.
English: I now rejoice, not that you were grieved, but that you were grieved to repentance. For you were grieved in a godly way, that you might suffer loss by us in nothing.

Kikuyu: Kĩeha kĩrĩa kiumanĩte kũrĩ Ngai kĩrehaga ũhoro wa kũhera kũrĩa gũtũmaga andũ mahonoke, na gũtirĩ wĩriraga gwĩka ũguo, no kĩeha kĩa maũndũ ma thĩ kĩrehaga gĩkuũ.
English: For godly sorrow produces repentance leading to salvation, which brings no regret. But the sorrow of the world produces death.

Kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, ta kĩonei kĩeha kĩu kiumanĩte na Ngai kĩrĩa gĩciarĩte thĩinĩ wanyu: nĩgĩtũmĩte mũgĩe na kĩyo kĩnene, o na wendo mũnene wa gwĩkũũra, na gwakanwo nĩ ngoro, na kĩmako, na kwĩrirĩria kũnene, na kwĩrũmbũiya, o na wetereri wa kuona atĩ kĩhooto nĩkĩahingio. Maũndũ-inĩ mothe-rĩ, nĩmwĩonanĩtie inyuĩ ene atĩ mũtirĩ na ũcuuke ũndũ-inĩ ũyũ.
English: For behold, this same thing, that you were grieved in a godly way, what earnest care it worked in you. Yes, what defense, indignation, fear, longing, zeal, and vindication! In everything you demonstrated yourselves to be pure in the matter.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, o na gwatuĩka nĩndamwandĩkĩire marũa-rĩ, ndiamaandĩkire nĩ ũndũ wa mũndũ ũrĩa wehĩtie, kana nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa wahĩtĩirio, no nĩgeetha mũhote kũmenya inyuĩ ene mũrĩ mbere ya Ngai ũrĩa mwĩheanĩte nĩ ũndũ witũ.
English: So although I wrote to you, I wrote not for his cause that did the wrong, nor for his cause that suffered the wrong, but that your earnest care for us might be revealed in you in the sight of God.

Kikuyu: Maũndũ maya mothe-rĩ, nĩmo matũmĩte tũũmĩrĩrio. O ũndũ ũmwe na kũũmĩrĩrio gwitũ-rĩ, makĩria nĩtwakenire nĩ ũndũ wa kuona ũrĩa Tito aakenete, tondũ roho wake nĩwekĩrĩtwo hinya nĩ inyuĩ inyuothe.
English: Therefore we have been comforted. In our comfort we rejoiced the more exceedingly for the joy of Titus, because his spirit has been refreshed by you all.

Kikuyu: Nĩnderahĩire Tito nĩ ũndũ wanyu, na inyuĩ mũtianjonorire. No o ta ũrĩa maũndũ mothe marĩa twamwĩrire maarĩ ma ma-rĩ, noguo kwĩraha gwitũ nĩ ũndũ wanyu harĩ Tito o nakuo gũtuĩkĩte ũhoro wa ma.
English: For if in anything I have boasted to him on your behalf, I was not disappointed. But as we spoke all things to you in truth, so our glorying also which I made before Titus was found to be truth.

Kikuyu: Naguo wendo ũrĩa amwendete naguo nĩũkĩrĩrĩirie kũneneha aaririkana ũrĩa inyuothe mwaathĩkire, na ũrĩa mwamwamũkĩrire mũrĩ na gwĩtigĩra na kũinaina.
English: His affection is more abundantly toward you, while he remembers all of your obedience, how with fear and trembling you received him.

Kikuyu: Ndĩ na gĩkeno tondũ rĩu ndĩ na mwĩhoko mũkinyanĩru thĩinĩ wanyu.
English: I rejoice that in everything I am confident concerning you.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, nĩtũkwenda mũmenye ũhoro wa Wega wa Ngai ũrĩa aheanĩte makanitha-inĩ ma Makedonia.
English: Moreover, brothers, we make known to you the grace of God which has been given in the assemblies of Macedonia,

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa andũ acio maageririo na magerio manene-rĩ, gĩkeno kĩao kĩnene na ũthĩĩni wao mũkĩru nĩciatũmire maingĩhĩrwo nĩ ũtaana mũnene.
English: how in a severe ordeal of affliction, the abundance of their joy and their deep poverty abounded to the riches of their generosity.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo niĩ ndĩ mũira atĩ maaheanire indo o ta ũrĩa maahotire, o na makĩruta makĩria ma ũrĩa mangĩahotire, nĩ ũndũ wa kwĩyendera kwao.
English: For according to their power, I testify, yes and beyond their power, they gave of their own accord,

Kikuyu: Nao magĩtũthaitha na hinya mũno metĩkĩrio kũgwatanĩra ũhoro-inĩ wa gũteithia andũ arĩa aamũre.
English: begging us with much entreaty to receive this grace and the fellowship in the service to the saints.

Kikuyu: Magĩĩka ũndũ ũcio matiawĩkire ta ũrĩa twerĩgĩrĩire, no maambire kwĩheana o ene kũrĩ Mwathani o na kũrĩ ithuĩ, kũringana na ũrĩa Ngai endete.
English: This was not as we had expected, but first they gave their own selves to the Lord, and to us through the will of God.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩtwaringĩrĩirie Tito, kuona atĩ nĩwe wambĩrĩirie ũndũ ũcio, atuĩke wa kũrĩĩkia ũndũ ũcio wa ũtugi wanyu.
English: So we urged Titus, that as he had made a beginning before, so he would also complete in you this grace.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa mũgaacagĩra harĩ maũndũ mothe, nĩmo gwĩtĩkia, na ũhoro wa kwaria, na wa ũmenyo, na thĩinĩ wa kĩyo gĩkinyanĩru, o na wa wendo wanyu harĩ ithuĩ-rĩ, kĩingĩhiei wega ũyũ wa gũtugana o naguo.
English: But as you abound in everything—in faith, utterance, knowledge, all earnestness, and in your love to us—see that you also abound in this grace.

Kikuyu: Ti kũmwatha ndĩramwatha, no ndĩrenda kũgeria wendani wanyu, nyone ũrĩa ũrĩ wa ma ngĩũgerekania na kĩyo kĩa andũ arĩa angĩ.
English: I speak not by way of commandment, but as proving through the earnestness of others the sincerity also of your love.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩmũũĩ ũhoro wa wega wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ atĩ, o na gũtuĩka aarĩ gĩtonga, nĩatuĩkire mũthĩĩni nĩ ũndũ wanyu, nĩgeetha inyuĩ mũtuĩke itonga na ũndũ wa ũrĩa aathĩĩnĩkĩte.
English: For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, that you through his poverty might become rich.

Kikuyu: Ha ũhoro ũyũ-rĩ, mataaro makwa ma ũndũ ũrĩa ũmwagĩrĩire makĩria nĩ atĩrĩ: Mwaka ũcio ũrathirire nĩ inyuĩ mwarĩ a mbere, na to ũndũ wa kũheana gwiki, no o na nĩ ũndũ wa kwĩrirĩria gwĩka ũguo.
English: I give advice in this: it is expedient for you who were the first to start a year ago, not only to do, but also to be willing.

Kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, kĩrĩĩkiei wĩra ũcio, nĩgeetha kĩyo kĩanyu kĩa wendo wa gwĩka ũguo kĩiganane na kũrĩĩkia kwanyu kwa ũndũ ũcio, kũringana na kĩrĩa mũrĩ nakĩo.
English: But now complete the doing also, that as there was the readiness to be willing, so there may be the completion also out of your ability.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩkorwo arĩ na wendo, kĩheo kĩu nĩgĩtĩkĩrĩku kũringana na ũrĩa mũndũ arĩ na indo, ti kũringana na ũrĩa aciagĩte.
English: For if the readiness is there, it is acceptable according to what you have, not according to what you don’t have.

Kikuyu: Kwĩrirĩria gwitũ ti amwe mahũthĩrio mũrigo na inyuĩ mũhinyĩrĩrio, no nĩgeetha andũ othe maigananio.
English: For this is not that others may be eased and you distressed,

Kikuyu: Ihinda rĩĩrĩ mũingĩhĩtie indo-rĩ, nĩigũteithia wagi wao, nĩgeetha rĩrĩa magaakorwo na indo o nao magaateithia wagi wanyu. Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ othe nĩmakaigananio,
English: but for equality. Your abundance at this present time supplies their lack, that their abundance also may become a supply for your lack, that there may be equality.

Kikuyu: o ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Mũndũ ũrĩa wonganĩtie nyingĩ, ndarĩ kĩndũ aatigĩirio; nake ũrĩa wonganirie nini, ndarĩ kĩndũ aagire.”
English: As it is written, “He who gathered much had nothing left over, and he who gathered little had no lack.”*

Kikuyu: Nĩngũcookeria Ngai ngaatho, ũrĩa wekĩrire ngoro-inĩ ya Tito ũhoro wa kũmũrũmbũiya o ta ũrĩa niĩ ndĩ na ũhoro ũcio.
English: But thanks be to God, who puts the same earnest care for you into the heart of Titus.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Tito to mathaithana maitũ aamũkĩrire, no nĩegũũka kũrĩ inyuĩ arĩ na kĩyo kĩnene nĩ ũndũ wa kwĩyendera gwake.
English: For he indeed accepted our exhortation, but being himself very earnest, he went out to you of his own accord.

Kikuyu: O na nĩtũgũtũma Tito marĩ hamwe na mũrũ wa Ithe witũ ũrĩa ũrĩ na ngumo njega makanitha-inĩ mothe nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa atungatagĩra Ũhoro-ũrĩa- Mwega.
English: We have sent together with him the brother whose praise in the Good News is known throughout all the assemblies.

Kikuyu: Makĩria ma ũguo, nĩwe wathuurirwo nĩ makanitha nĩguo atwarane na ithuĩ ũhoro-inĩ wa mũhothi ũcio wa ũtaana, o ũcio tũrutagĩra wĩra nĩguo tũgoocithie Mwathani we mwene, na tuonanie kĩyo kĩrĩa tũrĩ nakĩo gĩa kwenda gũteithia.
English: Not only so, but he was also appointed by the assemblies to travel with us in this grace, which is served by us to the glory of the Lord himself, and to show our readiness.

Kikuyu: Nĩtũrageria mũno kweherera ũndũ o wothe ũngĩtũma tũrutwo mahĩtia rĩrĩa tũkũramata kĩheo gĩkĩ kĩheanĩtwo na ũtaana.
English: We are avoiding this, that any man should blame us concerning this abundance which is administered by us.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩtũrerutanĩria gwĩka maũndũ marĩa mangĩonwo magĩrĩire mbere ya Mwathani, na to harĩ we wiki, no o na ningĩ monwo marĩ magĩrĩru nĩ andũ o nao.
English: Having regard for honorable things, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the sight of men.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ hamwe nao, nĩtũgũtũma mũrũ wa Ithe witũ ũngĩ, ũrĩa tuonete maita maingĩ na njĩra nyingĩ atĩ arĩ na kĩyo, o na arĩ nakĩo makĩria nĩ ũndũ wa mwĩhoko wake mũnene harĩ inyuĩ.
English: We have sent with them our brother whom we have many times proved earnest in many things, but now much more earnest, by reason of the great confidence which he has in you.

Kikuyu: Naguo ũhoro wa Tito-rĩ, nĩ mũthiritũ wakwa ũrĩa tũrutithanagia wĩra nake nĩ ũndũ wanyu; na ha ũhoro ũkoniĩ ariũ a Ithe witũ-rĩ, o nĩ atũmwo a makanitha na monanagia ũgooci wa Kristũ.
English: As for Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker for you. As for our brothers, they are the apostles of the assemblies, the glory of Christ.

Kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio oniai andũ acio ũira wa wendani wanyu, na gĩtũmi kĩrĩa gĩtũmaga twĩrahe nĩ ũndũ wanyu nĩguo makanitha mone ũndũ ũcio.
English: Therefore show the proof of your love to them before the assemblies, and of our boasting on your behalf.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ndikuona harĩ bata ndĩmwandĩkĩre ũhoro wa ũteithio ũrĩa wagĩrĩire kũheo andũ arĩa aamũre.
English: It is indeed unnecessary for me to write to you concerning the service to the saints,

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩnjũũĩ kĩyo kĩanyu gĩa kwenda gũteithia, na nĩngoretwo ngĩĩrahĩra ũndũ ũcio kũrĩ andũ a Makedonia, ngameera atĩ kuuma mwaka ũcio ũrathirire, inyuĩ andũ a Akaia mũtũire mwĩhaarĩirie kũheana; na kĩyo kĩu kĩanyu nĩkĩo kĩarahũrĩte aingĩ ao.
English: for I know your readiness, of which I boast on your behalf to those of Macedonia, that Achaia has been prepared for the past year. Your zeal has stirred up very many of them.

Kikuyu: No nĩngũtũma ariũ a Ithe witũ nĩguo kwĩraha gwitũ nĩ ũndũ wanyu ũhoro-inĩ ũyũ wa mũhothi gũtigatuĩke gwa tũhũ, no mũgaakorwo mwĩhaarĩirie, o ta ũrĩa ndoigire atĩ nĩmwĩharĩirie.
English: But I have sent the brothers so that our boasting on your behalf may not be in vain in this respect, that, just as I said, you may be prepared,

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo andũ amwe a Makedonia mangĩrehane na niĩ makore mũtehaarĩirie-rĩ, ithuĩ, o na tũtekũmũgweta-rĩ, nĩtũgaaconoka nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo tũmwĩhokeete.
English: lest by any means, if anyone from Macedonia comes there with me and finds you unprepared, we (to say nothing of you) would be disappointed in this confident boasting.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, nĩndĩronire arĩ wega gũthaitha ariũ a Ithe witũ maambe mamũceerere ihinda rĩu rĩtanakinya, nĩguo marĩkĩrĩrie mĩbango ya kĩheo kĩa ũtaana kĩrĩa mweranĩire. Hĩndĩ ĩyo kĩheo kĩu nĩgĩgakorwo kĩrĩ ho, na kĩheanĩtwo na kwĩyendera, no ti na kũringĩrĩrio.
English: I thought it necessary therefore to entreat the brothers that they would go before to you and arrange ahead of time the generous gift that you promised before, that the same might be ready as a matter of generosity, and not of greediness.

Kikuyu: Ririkanai ũũ: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũhaandaga tũnini akaagetha o tũnini, nake ũrĩa ũhaandaga nyingĩ akaagetha nyingĩ.
English: Remember this: he who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly. He who sows bountifully will also reap bountifully.

Kikuyu: O mũndũ aagĩrĩirwo nĩkũheana kĩrĩa atuĩte na ngoro yake nĩekũheana, na ndakaheane atekwenda kana taarĩ kũringĩrĩrio, nĩgũkorwo Ngai endaga mũndũ ũrĩa ũheanaga akenete.
English: Let each man give according as he has determined in his heart, not grudgingly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.

Kikuyu: Nake Ngai arĩ na ũhoti wa gũtũma mũingĩhĩrwo nĩ maũndũ mothe ma wega wake, nĩguo maũndũ-inĩ mothe na mahinda-inĩ mothe, mũkoragwo na kĩrĩa gĩothe mũbatarĩtio nĩkĩo, nĩguo mũkoragwo mũingĩhĩtie indo cia kũruta wĩra o wothe mwega.
English: And God is able to make all grace abound to you, that you, always having all sufficiency in everything, may abound to every good work.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Nĩahurunjĩte iheo ciake akahe andũ arĩa athĩĩni; ũthingu wake ũtũũraga nginya tene na tene.”
English: As it is written, “He has scattered abroad. He has given to the poor. His righteousness remains forever.”*

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ũrĩa ũheaga mũhandi mbeũ na akamũhe irio cia kũrĩa, nĩarĩmũheaga mbeũ cia kũhaanda, na atũme cingĩhe, na ongerere magetha ma ũthingu wanyu.
English: Now may he who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food, supply and multiply your seed for sowing, and increase the fruits of your righteousness,

Kikuyu: Na inyuĩ nĩmũrĩtongagio na njĩra ciothe nĩguo mũheanage na ũtaana hĩndĩ ciothe, naguo ũtaana wanyu, nĩ ũndũ wa wĩra witũ, ũtũmage Ngai acookagĩrio ngaatho.
English: you being enriched in everything for all generosity, which produces thanksgiving to God through us.

Kikuyu: Ũteithio ũyũ mũraruta ti kuuga atĩ no andũ a Ngai mũrahingĩria mabata mao, no nĩũtũmaga Ngai acookagĩrio ngaatho na njĩra nyingĩ.
English: For this service of giving that you perform not only makes up for lack among the saints, but abounds also through much giving of thanks to God,

Kikuyu: Na tondũ wa ũtungata ũcio mwĩonanĩtie naguo ũrĩa mũhaana-rĩ, andũ nĩmarĩgoocaga Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa mwathĩkĩire Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega wa Kristũ ũrĩa muumbũraga atĩ nĩmũwĩtĩkĩtie-rĩ, na nĩ ũndũ wa ũtaana wanyu wakũgwatanĩra nao o na wakũgwatanĩra na andũ arĩa angĩ othe.
English: seeing that through the proof given by this service, they glorify God for the obedience of your confession to the Good News of Christ and for the generosity of your contribution to them and to all,

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa mekũmũhoera, ngoro ciao nĩimwĩciiragia, tondũ wa ũrĩa wega wa Ngai ũrĩa inyuĩ mũheetwo ũkĩrĩrĩirie kũingĩha.
English: while they themselves also, with supplication on your behalf, yearn for you by reason of the exceeding grace of God in you.

Kikuyu: Ngai arocookerio ngaatho nĩ ũndũ wa kĩheo gĩake kĩrĩa gĩtangiugĩka na ciugo ũrĩa gĩtariĩ!
English: Now thanks be to God for his unspeakable gift!

Kikuyu: Niĩ Paũlũ nĩndamũthaitha nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa Kristũ arĩ mũhooreri na mũcaayanĩri, o niĩ ngoragwo na guoya rĩrĩa ndĩ na inyuĩ, no ngakorwo na ũcamba rĩrĩa itarĩ na inyuĩ!
English: Now I Paul, myself, entreat you by the humility and gentleness of Christ, I who in your presence am lowly among you, but being absent am bold toward you.

Kikuyu: Nĩndamũthaitha mũtikanatũmerĩrĩangookangaakorwonaũcambataũrĩanjĩrĩgĩrĩiregũkorwonaguonĩũndũwaandũ amwe arĩa meciiragia atĩ mũtũũrĩre ũrĩa tũtũũraga naguo nĩ wa gũkũ thĩ.
English: Yes, I beg you that I may not, when present, show courage with the confidence with which I intend to be bold against some, who consider us to be walking according to the flesh.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o na gũtuĩka tũtũũraga thĩ ĩno, tũtirũaga mbaara ta ũrĩa andũ a thĩ marũaga.
English: For though we walk in the flesh, we don’t wage war according to the flesh;

Kikuyu: Matharaita marĩa tũrũaga namo ti ma thĩ ĩno, no nĩ marĩa marĩ na hinya wa Ngai wa kũmomora ciĩgitĩro cia hinya.
English: for the weapons of our warfare are not of the flesh, but mighty before God to the throwing down of strongholds,

Kikuyu: Nĩtũmomoraga ngarari, na ũndũ o wothe wa mwĩtũũgĩrio wa ngoro ũrĩa ũngĩgirĩrĩria ũhoro wa kũmenya Ngai, na tũgataha meciiria o mothe nĩguo matuĩke mĩgwate ya gwathĩkagĩra Kristũ.
English: throwing down imaginations and every high thing that is exalted against the knowledge of God and bringing every thought into captivity to the obedience of Christ,

Kikuyu: Na nĩtwĩhaarĩirie kũherithia gĩĩko o gĩothe kĩa ũremi, rĩrĩa mũgaakorwo mũtuĩkĩte a gwathĩka biũ.
English: and being in readiness to avenge all disobedience when your obedience is made full.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ mũrarora maũndũ o igũrũ. Mũndũ o wothe angĩkorwo arĩ na ma atĩ nĩ wa Kristũ-rĩ, aagĩrĩire eciirie o rĩngĩ atĩ o na ithuĩ tũrĩ a Kristũ o ta ũrĩa o nake arĩ wake.
English: Do you look at things only as they appear in front of your face? If anyone trusts in himself that he is Christ’s, let him consider this again with himself, that even as he is Christ’s, so we also are Christ’s.

Kikuyu: No kuoneke ta ndĩreraha mũno nĩ ũndũ wa ũhoti ũrĩa Mwathani aatũheire. No rĩrĩ, ũhoti witũ nĩ wa kũmuongerera hinya na ti wa kũmũhinyĩrĩria. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndingĩconoka nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩka ũguo.
English: For even if I boast somewhat abundantly concerning our authority, which the Lord gave for building you up and not for casting you down, I will not be ashamed,

Kikuyu: Ndikwenda kuonwo ta ndĩrenda kũmũmakia na marũa makwa.
English: that I may not seem as if I desire to terrify you by my letters.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo andũ amwe moigaga atĩrĩ, “Marũa make marĩ na ũritũ mũingĩ, na marĩ na hinya, no rĩrĩa arĩ hamwe na ithuĩ-rĩ, ti mũndũ ũngĩtĩĩka, nayo mĩario yake nĩ ya tũhũ.”
English: For, “His letters”, they say, “are weighty and strong, but his bodily presence is weak, and his speech is despised.”

Kikuyu: Andũ ta acio magĩrĩirwo nĩkũmenya atĩ o ũrĩa ũhoro witũ ũkoragwo ũrĩ marũa-inĩ maitũ rĩrĩa tũtarĩ ho-rĩ, ũguo noguo tũkoragwo tũhaana tũgĩĩka maũndũ rĩrĩa tũrĩ ho.
English: Let such a person consider this, that what we are in word by letters when we are absent, such are we also in deed when we are present.

Kikuyu: Tũtigeragia kwĩiganania kana kwĩgerekania na amwe arĩa megathagĩrĩria. Rĩrĩa megwĩthima na arĩa mahaana tao, na makegerekania na arĩa matariĩ tao-rĩ, matirĩ ũndũ mooĩ.
English: For we are not bold to number or compare ourselves with some of those who commend themselves. But they themselves, measuring themselves by themselves, and comparing themselves with themselves, are without understanding.

Kikuyu: No ithuĩ- rĩ, tũtingĩĩraha makĩria ma ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire, no tũkwĩraha kũringana na gĩcunjĩ kĩa wĩra ũrĩa Ngai atũheete, o na inyuĩ mũrĩ gĩcunjĩ kĩa wĩra ũcio witũ.
English: But we will not boast beyond proper limits, but within the boundaries with which God appointed to us, which reach even to you.

Kikuyu: Tũtireraha gũkĩra ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire, ta ũrĩa kũngĩrĩ korwo tũtiokire kũrĩ inyuĩ, nĩgũkorwo nĩ ithuĩ twamũkinyĩire na Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega wa Kristũ.
English: For we don’t stretch ourselves too much, as though we didn’t reach to you. For we came even as far as to you with the Good News of Christ,

Kikuyu: O na tũtiĩrahaga gũkĩra ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire nĩ ũndũ wa wĩra mũrute nĩ andũ angĩ. Ũndũ ũrĩa twĩrĩgĩrĩire nĩ atĩ, o ũrĩa wĩtĩkio wanyu ũrathiĩ na mbere gũkũra, nakĩo gĩcunjĩ kĩrĩa tũrĩ nakĩo thĩinĩ wanyu nĩgĩkwarama mũno,
English: not boasting beyond proper limits in other men’s labors, but having hope that as your faith grows, we will be abundantly enlarged by you in our sphere of influence,

Kikuyu: nĩgeetha tũhote kũhunjia Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega ngʼongo iria irĩ mbere ya kwanyu. Nĩgũkorwo tũtikwenda kwĩraha nĩ ũndũ wa wĩra ũrutĩtwo rũgongo rũngĩ nĩ mũndũ ũngĩ.
English: so as to preach the Good News even to the parts beyond you, not to boast in what someone else has already done.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũrĩa ũkwĩraha, nĩerahage thĩinĩ wa Mwathani.”
English: But “he who boasts, let him boast in the Lord.”*

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũndũ ũrĩa wĩgaathagĩrĩria tiwe wonagwo arĩ mwega, no nĩ ũrĩa Mwathani agathagĩrĩria.
English: For it isn’t he who commends himself who is approved, but whom the Lord commends.

Kikuyu: Nĩngwĩhoka nĩmũgũkirĩrĩria ũrimũ ũrĩa mũnini ndĩ naguo; ndamũthaitha mũngirĩrĩrie.
English: I wish that you would bear with me in a little foolishness, but indeed you do bear with me.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩraigua ũiru nĩ ũndũ wanyu, ũiru uumanĩte na ũngai. Ndamwĩrĩire o mũthuuri ũmwe, na nĩwe Kristũ, nĩgeetha ngaamũneana kũrĩ we mũrĩ ta mũirĩtu gathirange.
English: For I am jealous over you with a godly jealousy. For I promised you in marriage to one husband, that I might present you as a pure virgin to Christ.

Kikuyu: No ndĩ na guoya gũtigatuĩke atĩ meciiria manyu no maturuurio mũtiganĩrie ũgima na ũtheru wa kwĩrutĩra Kristũ, o ta ũrĩa Hawa aaheenekire nĩ nyoka na wara wayo.
English: But I am afraid that somehow, as the serpent deceived Eve in his craftiness, so your minds might be corrupted from the simplicity that is in Christ.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ nĩmwamũkagĩra ũhoro ũngĩ mũkenete, mũndũ o wothe angĩmũhunjĩria ũhoro wa Jesũ ũngĩ tiga Jesũ ũrĩa twamũhunjĩirie, kana mũngĩamũkĩra roho ũngĩ tiga Roho ũrĩa mwamũkĩrire, o na kana mũgetĩkia ũhoro-mwega ũngĩ tiga ũrĩa mwetĩkirie.
English: For if he who comes preaches another Jesus whom we didn’t preach, or if you receive a different spirit which you didn’t receive, or a different “good news” which you didn’t accept, you put up with that well enough.

Kikuyu: No ndigwĩciiria atĩ ndĩ mũnini o na hanini kũrĩ “atũmwo acio anene.”
English: For I reckon that I am not at all behind the very best apostles.

Kikuyu: No ngorwo itarĩ mũrute ũhoro-inĩ wa kwaria, no ndĩ na ũmenyo. Nĩtũmũmenyithĩtie maũndũ maya wega na njĩra ciothe.
English: But though I am unskilled in speech, yet I am not unskilled in knowledge. No, in every way we have been revealed to you in all things.

Kikuyu: Hihi nĩ kwĩhia ndeehirie nĩ kwĩnyiihia nĩguo ndĩmũtũũgĩrie inyuĩ na ũndũ wa kũmũhunjĩria Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega wa Ngai hatarĩ irĩhi?
English: Or did I commit a sin in humbling myself that you might be exalted, because I preached to you God’s Good News free of charge?

Kikuyu: Nĩgũtunya ndaatunyaga makanitha mangĩ ngĩamũkĩra ũteithio kuuma kũrĩ o nĩguo hote kũmũtungata.
English: I robbed other assemblies, taking wages from them that I might serve you.

Kikuyu: Na hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndaarĩ na inyuĩ, ndabatario nĩ kĩndũ- rĩ, ndiatuĩkaga mũrigo harĩ mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ, nĩgũkorwo ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ moka kuuma Makedonia nĩmaheire indo cia kũigana mabataro makwa. Nĩndĩgirĩrĩirie ndigatuĩke mũrigo kũrĩ inyuĩ na njĩra o yothe, na nĩngũthiĩ na mbere gwĩka ũguo.
English: When I was present with you and was in need, I wasn’t a burden on anyone, for the brothers, when they came from Macedonia, supplied the measure of my need. In everything I kept myself from being burdensome to you, and I will continue to do so.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ o ta ũrĩa ũhoro wa ma wa Kristũ ũrĩ thĩinĩ wakwa, gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũ thĩinĩ wa ngʼongo cia Akaia ũngĩngiria ndĩrahe ũguo.
English: As the truth of Christ is in me, no one will stop me from this boasting in the regions of Achaia.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kĩĩ gĩatũma njuge ũguo? Nĩ tondũ ndimwendete? Ngai nĩoĩ atĩ nĩndĩmwendete!
English: Why? Because I don’t love you? God knows.

Kikuyu: Na nĩngũthiĩ na mbere na gwĩka ũrĩa ndĩreka, nĩguo njagithie kĩene andũ arĩa macaragia mweke wa kuoneka atĩ nĩmaiganaine na ithuĩ, thĩinĩ wa maũndũ marĩa merahaga namo.
English: But what I do, that I will continue to do, that I may cut off opportunity from those who desire an opportunity, that in which they boast, they may be recognized just like us.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo andũ ta acio nĩ atũmwo a maheeni, aruti wĩra a kũhĩtithia andũ, na metuaga atũmwo a Kristũ.
English: For such men are false apostles, deceitful workers, masquerading as Christ’s apostles.

Kikuyu: Naguo ũndũ ũcio ti wa kũmakania, nĩgũkorwo o na Shaitani o nake nĩetuaga mũraika wa ũtheri.
English: And no wonder, for even Satan masquerades as an angel of light.

Kikuyu: Ndũkĩrĩ ũndũ wa kũgegania ndungata ciake cingĩĩtua ndungata cia ũthingu. Marigĩrĩrio-inĩ makaagĩa na kĩrĩa kĩringaine na ciĩko ciao.
English: It is no great thing therefore if his servants also masquerade as servants of righteousness, whose end will be according to their works.

Kikuyu: Nĩnguuga o rĩngĩ atĩrĩ: Mũndũ ndakae kuona ta ndĩ kĩrimũ. No angĩkorwo nĩguo mũkuona-rĩ, kĩnyamũkĩrei o ta ũrĩa mũngĩamũkĩra kĩrimũ, nĩguo ndĩĩrahe o hanini.
English: I say again, let no one think me foolish. But if so, yet receive me as foolish, that I also may boast a little.

Kikuyu: Ũhoro-inĩ wa mwĩraho ũyũ ndĩ naguo-rĩ, ndiraaria ta ũrĩa Mwathani angĩaria, no ndĩraaria o ta ũrĩa kĩrimũ kĩngĩaria.
English: That which I speak, I don’t speak according to the Lord, but as in foolishness, in this confidence of boasting.

Kikuyu: Kuona atĩ andũ aingĩ nĩmareraha ta ũrĩa thĩ ĩĩrahaga-rĩ, o na niĩ ngwĩraha o tao.
English: Seeing that many boast after the flesh, I will also boast.

Kikuyu: Tondũ inyuĩ mũkĩrĩ oogĩ-rĩ, nĩmũkiragĩrĩria arĩa irimũ mũkenete!
English: For you bear with the foolish gladly, being wise.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ, nĩmũkiragĩrĩria mũndũ o wothe o na angĩmũtua ngombo ciake, kana ũrĩa ũkũmũhatĩrĩria, kana ũrĩa ũkũrĩa indo cianyu, kana ũrĩa ũgwĩtũũgĩria mbere yanyu, o na kana ũrĩa ũkũmũhũũra hĩ.
English: For you bear with a man if he brings you into bondage, if he devours you, if he takes you captive, if he exalts himself, or if he strikes you on the face.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũconoka ngiuga atĩ ithuĩ nĩtwaremirwo nĩ gwĩka maũndũ ta macio! Ũndũ ũrĩa mũndũ ũngĩ angĩhota kwĩraha nĩ ũndũ waguo-rĩ, ndĩkĩarie ta ndĩ kĩrimũ-rĩ, o na niĩ no ngĩgerie kwĩraha nĩ ũndũ waguo.
English: To my shame, I speak as though we had been weak. Yet in whatever way anyone is bold (I speak in foolishness), I am bold also.

Kikuyu: Andũ acio nĩ Ahibirania? O na niĩ ndĩ Mũhibirania. Andũ acio nĩ Aisiraeli? O na niĩ ndĩ Mũisiraeli. Andũ acio nĩ njiaro cia Iburahĩmu? O na niĩ ndĩ wa rũciaro rwa Iburahĩmu.
English: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they the offspring† of Abraham? So am I.

Kikuyu: Andũ acio nĩ ndungata cia Kristũ? (Ngwaria ũguo ta ndĩ mũgũrũku.) Niĩ nĩndĩmakĩrĩte. Nĩndutĩte wĩra na kĩyo kũmakĩra, na ngaikio njeera mahinda maingĩ makĩria yao, na ngahũũrwo na kĩboko makĩria, o na mahinda maingĩ ndanakuhĩrĩria gũkua.
English: Are they servants of Christ? (I speak as one beside himself.) I am more so: in labors more abundantly, in prisons more abundantly, in stripes above measure, and in deaths often.

Kikuyu: Ayahudi nĩmahũũrire na kĩboko maita matano, o ihinda iboko mĩrongo ĩtatũ na kenda.
English: Five times I received forty stripes minus one from the Jews.

Kikuyu: Nĩndahũũrirwo na thanju maita matatũ, na riita rĩmwe ngĩhũũrwo na mahiga nyuguto,* na maita matatũ ndanathũkĩrwo nĩ marikabu ndĩ thĩinĩ, na ngĩraara o na ngĩtinda iria-inĩ ũtukũ ũmwe na mũthenya mũgima.
English: Three times I was beaten with rods. Once I was stoned. Three times I suffered shipwreck. I have been a night and a day in the deep.

Kikuyu: Ngoretwo ngĩthiĩ ngʼendo nyingĩ. Ngagĩkorwo ndĩ ũgwati-inĩ wa gũtwarwo nĩ njũũĩ, na ũgwati-inĩ wa atunyani, na ũgwati-inĩ wa gũũkĩrĩrwo nĩ andũ a rũrĩrĩ rwitũ, na ũgwati-inĩ wa gũũkĩrĩrwo nĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ; na ngoona ũgwati ndĩ matũũra-inĩ manene, na ndĩ thĩinĩ wa bũrũri, na ndĩ iria-inĩ; o na ũgwati wa andũ arĩa maheenanagia atĩ nĩ ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ.
English: I have been in travels often, perils of rivers, perils of robbers, perils from my countrymen, perils from the Gentiles, perils in the city, perils in the wilderness, perils in the sea, perils among false brothers;

Kikuyu: Ndaanaruta wĩra mũritũ na wa mĩnoga, na maita maingĩ ngaagaga toro; ndaanakorwo na ngʼaragu na nyoota, na maita maingĩ ngaaga kĩndũ gĩa kũrĩa; ndaanakorwo heho-inĩ na ndĩ njaga.
English: in labor and travail, in watchings often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, and in cold and nakedness.

Kikuyu: Hamwe na macio mothe-rĩ, o mũthenya ngoragwo na mũrigo, ngĩĩtanga nĩ ũndũ wa makanitha mothe.
English: Besides those things that are outside, there is that which presses on me daily: anxiety for all the assemblies.

Kikuyu: Nũũ ũngĩkorwo aagĩte hinya, na niĩ njage gũkorwo njũrĩtwo nĩ hinya? Nũũ ũngĩhĩngithio, na niĩ njage gũkorwo ngĩcinwo nĩ marakara?
English: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is caused to stumble, and I don’t burn with indignation?

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo no nginya ndĩĩrahe, ngwĩrahĩra maũndũ marĩa monanagia ũrĩa itarĩ na hinya.
English: If I must boast, I will boast of the things that concern my weakness.

Kikuyu: Ngai, na nowe Ithe wa Mwathani Jesũ, ũrĩa ũgoocagwo nginya tene-rĩ, nĩoĩ atĩ ndiraheenania.
English: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus Christ, he who is blessed forever more, knows that I don’t lie.

Kikuyu: Kũu Dameski, barũthi ũrĩa waathanaga arĩ rungu rwa Mũthamaki Areta-rĩ, nĩathanire itũũra inene rĩa andũ a Dameski rĩrangĩrwo nĩgeetha aanyiitithie.
English: In Damascus the governor under King Aretas guarded the Damascenes’ city, desiring to arrest me.

Kikuyu: No nĩndoimĩirio ndirica, ngĩikũrũkĩrio rũthingo- inĩ ndĩ thĩinĩ wa gĩkabũ, ngĩhonoka kũnyiitwo nĩwe.
English: I was let down in a basket through a window by the wall, and escaped his hands.

Kikuyu: No nginya thiĩ na mbere na kwĩraha. O na gũtuĩka gwĩka ũguo gũtirĩ uumithio-rĩ, rĩu nĩngwaria ũhoro wa maũndũ marĩa ndanonio nĩ Mwathani, na marĩa ndanaguũrĩrio nĩwe.
English: It is doubtless not profitable for me to boast, but I will come to visions and revelations of the Lord.

Kikuyu: Nĩnjũũĩ mũndũ thĩinĩ wa Kristũ, ũrĩa woirwo mĩaka ikũmi na ĩna mĩthiru, agĩtwarwo igũrũ rĩa gatatũ. Kana oirwo arĩ na mwĩrĩ kana atarĩ naguo, niĩ ndiũĩ, Ngai nĩwe ũũĩ.
English: I know a man in Christ who was caught up into the third heaven fourteen years ago—whether in the body, I don’t know, or whether out of the body, I don’t know; God knows.

Kikuyu: Na nĩnjũũĩ atĩ mũndũ ũcio, kana aarĩ mwĩrĩ-inĩ kana ndaarĩ mwĩrĩ-inĩ-rĩ, niĩ ndiũĩ, no Ngai nĩwe ũũĩ,
English: I know such a man (whether in the body, or outside of the body, I don’t know; God knows),

Kikuyu: nĩoirwo agĩtwarwo kũrĩa andũ arĩa metĩkĩtie mathiiaga, akĩigua maũndũ matangĩĩranĩka nĩ mũndũ, na maũndũ marĩa mũndũ atangĩtĩkĩrio kwaria.
English: how he was caught up into Paradise and heard unspeakable words, which it is not lawful for a man to utter.

Kikuyu: Nĩngwĩraha nĩ ũndũ wa mũndũ ta ũcio, no ndikwĩraha nĩ ũndũ wakwa niĩ mwene, tiga no ndĩĩrahire nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa njagĩte hinya.
English: On behalf of such a one I will boast, but on my own behalf I will not boast, except in my weaknesses.

Kikuyu: O na ingĩenda kwĩraha-rĩ, ndingĩtuĩka kĩrimũ, nĩ ũndũ ingĩkorwo ngĩaria ũhoro wa ma. No ndigwĩka ũguo, nĩguo gũtikagĩe mũndũ ũgwĩciiria ũhoro wakwa makĩria ma ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa njĩkaga kana ũrĩa njugaga.
English: For if I would desire to boast, I will not be foolish; for I will speak the truth. But I refrain, so that no man may think more of me than that which he sees in me or hears from me.

Kikuyu: Nĩguo ndikanae gwĩtũũgĩria nĩ ũndũ wa ũguũrio mũnene ũguo-rĩ, nĩndekĩrirwo mũigua thĩinĩ wa mwĩrĩ wakwa, ũrĩ mũtũmwo wa Shaitani, nĩguo ũũnyariirage.
English: By reason of the exceeding greatness of the revelations, that I should not be exalted excessively, a thorn in the flesh was given to me: a messenger of Satan to torment me, that I should not be exalted excessively.

Kikuyu: Nĩndathaithire Mwathani mahinda matatũ anjehererie mũigua ũcio.
English: Concerning this thing, I begged the Lord three times that it might depart from me.

Kikuyu: Nowe akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Wega wakwa nĩũkũiganĩte, nĩgũkorwo hinya wakwa nĩũtuĩkaga mũkinyanĩru harĩa hatarĩ na hinya.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩngwĩraha o na makĩria ngenete mũno nĩ ũndũ wa kwaga hinya gwakwa, nĩgeetha hinya wa Kristũ ũikarage igũrũ rĩakwa.
English: He has said to me, “My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.” Most gladly therefore I will rather glory in my weaknesses, that the power of Christ may rest on me.

Kikuyu: Kĩu nĩkĩo gĩtũmaga ngenagĩre kwaga hinya, na irumi, na ũritũ wa mũtũũrĩre, na kũnyariirwo, o na mathĩĩna nĩ ũndũ wa Kristũ. Nĩgũkorwo hĩndĩ ĩrĩa njagĩte hinya, nĩguo ngoragwo na hinya.
English: Therefore I take pleasure in weaknesses, in injuries, in necessities, in persecutions, and in distresses, for Christ’s sake. For when I am weak, then am I strong.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩĩtuĩte kĩrimũ, no nĩ inyuĩ mwatũmire njĩke ũguo. Nĩ inyuĩ mwagĩrĩirwo nĩkũngaathĩrĩria, nĩgũkorwo niĩ ti niĩ mũnini mũno kũrĩ “atũmwo acio anene,” o na gũtuĩka niĩ ndĩkĩrĩ kĩndũ gĩtarĩ kĩene.
English: I have become foolish in boasting. You compelled me, for I ought to have been commended by you, for I am in no way inferior to the very best apostles, though I am nothing.

Kikuyu: Maũndũ marĩa matũmaga mũndũ amenywo atĩ nĩ mũtũmwo-rĩ, nĩmekirwo gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu na gũkirĩrĩria kũnene: gũkĩringwo ciama, na gũgĩĩkwo morirũ, na maũndũ ma kuonania hinya.
English: Truly the signs of an apostle were worked among you in all perseverance, in signs and wonders and mighty works.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ mwakĩrĩ anini gũkĩra makanitha marĩa mangĩ nakĩ, tiga o gũtuĩka atĩ niĩ ndiigana gũtuĩka mũrigo harĩ inyuĩ? Ndekerai ihĩtia rĩu!
English: For what is there in which you were made inferior to the rest of the assemblies, unless it is that I myself was not a burden to you? Forgive me this wrong!

Kikuyu: Rĩu nĩndĩhaarĩirie kũmũceerera ihinda rĩa gatatũ, na ndigũkorwo ndĩ mũrigo harĩ inyuĩ, tondũ ti indo cianyu ndĩrenda, no nĩ inyuĩ ndĩrenda. Tondũ-rĩ, ciana itiagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũigĩra aciari a cio mũthithũ, no aciari nĩo magĩrĩirwo nĩ kũigĩra ciana ciao mũthithũ.
English: Behold, this is the third time I am ready to come to you, and I will not be a burden to you; for I seek not your possessions, but you. For the children ought not to save up for the parents, but the parents for the children.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio no ngene kũhũthĩra kĩrĩa gĩothe ndĩ nakĩo nĩ ũndũ wanyu, o na niĩ mwene ndĩĩhũthĩre. Ingĩmwenda makĩria- rĩ, inyuĩ no mũkĩnyende o hanini?
English: I will most gladly spend and be spent for your souls. If I love you more abundantly, am I loved the less?

Kikuyu: No o na gũtariĩ o ũguo-rĩ, niĩ ndiatuĩkire mũrigo harĩ inyuĩ. No njĩĩrwo atĩ hihi ndaarĩ mwara, na atĩ nĩndamũheenirie!
English: Even so, I myself didn’t burden you. But you might say that being crafty, I caught you with deception.

Kikuyu: Hihi nĩndamũhatĩrĩirie na ũndũ wa mũndũ o na ũmwe wa arĩa ndamũtũmĩire?
English: Did I take advantage of you by anyone of those whom I have sent to you?

Kikuyu: Nĩndaringĩrĩirie Tito oke kũrĩ inyuĩ, na ngĩtũma mũrũ wa Ithe witũ moke nake. Tito ndaigana kũmũhatĩrĩria, kana nĩamũhatĩrĩirie? Githĩ tũtiarutire wĩra thĩinĩ wa roho o ro ũmwe, na tũkĩrũmĩrĩra njĩra o ro ĩmwe?
English: I exhorted Titus, and I sent the brother with him. Did Titus take any advantage of you? Didn’t we walk in the same spirit? Didn’t we walk in the same steps?

Kikuyu: Hihi ihinda rĩĩrĩ rĩothe mũkoretwo mũgĩĩciiria atĩ nĩ gwĩciirĩrĩra tũkoretwo tũgĩciirĩrĩra harĩ inyuĩ? Tũkoretwo tũkĩaria mbere ya Ngai ta andũ marĩ thĩinĩ wa Kristũ; na rĩrĩ, arata akwa nyenda, maũndũ marĩa mothe twĩkaga nĩ ma gũtũma inyuĩ muongererwo hinya.
English: Again, do you think that we are excusing ourselves to you? In the sight of God we speak in Christ. But all things, beloved, are for your edifying.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩndĩretigĩra, atĩ rĩrĩa ndĩrĩũka, ndahota gũkora mũtahaana ũrĩa ingĩenda mũkorwo mũhaana, na inyuĩ mwahota gũkoraitahaana ũrĩa mũngĩenda ngorwo haana. Ngetigĩra kwahota gũgaakorwo kũrĩ na ngũĩ, na ũiru, na marakara, na njatũkano, na njambanio, na njuukũ, na mwĩtĩĩo, o na kwaga kĩhaarĩro.
English: For I am afraid that perhaps when I come, I might find you not the way I want to, and that I might be found by you as you don’t desire, that perhaps there would be strife, jealousy, outbursts of anger, factions, slander, whisperings, proud thoughts, or riots,

Kikuyu: Ngwĩtigĩra atĩ rĩrĩa ngooka kũu rĩngĩ, Ngai wakwa ahota gũkanjonorithia mbere yanyu, na niĩ njigue kĩeha nĩ ũndũ wa andũ aingĩ arĩa meehirie o mbere, na matiĩrirĩte waganu wa maũndũ marĩa mooru, na ma ũmaraya, na maũndũ ma ũũra-thoni marĩa matũũrĩte meekaga.
English: that again when I come my God would humble me before you, and I would mourn for many of those who have sinned before now, and not repented of the uncleanness, sexual immorality, and lustfulness which they committed.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩ nĩ riita rĩa gatatũ gũũka kwanyu. Ũhoro o wothe no nginya ũrũgamĩrĩrwo nĩ ũira wa andũ eerĩ kana atatũ.
English: This is the third time I am coming to you. “At the mouth of two or three witnesses shall every word be established.”*

Kikuyu: Nĩndamũkaanirie hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndaarĩ na inyuĩ riita rĩa keerĩ. O na rĩu nĩngũmũkaania o rĩngĩ, o na itarĩ hamwe na inyuĩ: Rĩrĩa ndĩrĩũka rĩngĩ, andũ arĩa matũũrĩte mehagia kuuma tene, kana mũndũ ũngĩ o wothe, ndirĩ ngacaaĩra,
English: I have warned previously, and I warn again, as when I was present the second time, so now, being absent, I write to those who have sinned before now and to all the rest that if I come again, I will not spare,

Kikuyu: tondũ nĩmũrenda kũmenya kĩhooto kĩa atĩ nĩ Kristũ waragia na kanua gakwa. We ndamwĩkagĩra maũndũ ta atarĩ na hinya, no nĩonanagia atĩ arĩ na hinya gatagatĩ- inĩ kanyu.
English: seeing that you seek a proof of Christ who speaks in me who is not weak, but is powerful in you.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩ ma nĩambirwo mũtharaba-inĩ atarĩ na hinya, no nĩatũũraga muoyo nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wa Ngai. O ta ũguo-rĩ, o na ithuĩ tũtirĩ hinya tũrĩ thĩinĩ wake, no nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wa Ngai nĩtũtũũraga muoyo thĩinĩ wake nĩguo tũmũtungatage.
English: For he was crucified through weakness, yet he lives through the power of God. For we also are weak in him, but we will live with him through the power of God toward you.

Kikuyu: Mwĩgeriei, muone kana inyuĩ ene nĩ mwĩtĩkĩtie ũhoro wa ma; mwĩtuĩriei inyuĩ ene. Kaĩ inyuĩ mũtooĩ atĩ Kristũ Jesũ arĩ thĩinĩ wanyu, tiga no mũgĩkorirwo mũgwĩte kĩgeranio kĩu?
English: Examine your own selves, whether you are in the faith. Test your own selves. Or don’t you know about your own selves, that Jesus Christ is in you?—unless indeed you are disqualified.

Kikuyu: Na nĩngwĩhoka atĩ nĩmũrĩona atĩ ithuĩ tũtigwĩte kĩgeranio.
English: But I hope that you will know that we aren’t disqualified.

Kikuyu: Na rĩu tũkũhooya Ngai atĩ mũtikae gwĩka ũndũ o na ũmwe mũũru. Ti atĩ nĩguo andũ monage atĩ nĩtwĩtĩkĩrĩtwo nĩwe, na nĩtũtooranĩtie, no nĩgeetha inyuĩ mwĩkage maũndũ marĩa magĩrĩire, o na tũngĩoneka ta tũtatooranĩtie.
English: Now I pray to God that you do no evil; not that we may appear approved, but that you may do that which is honorable, though we may seem to have failed.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo tũtingĩhota gwĩka ũndũ ũreganĩte na ũhoro wa ma, tiga o ũrĩa ũrĩ wa ũhoro wa ma wiki.
English: For we can do nothing against the truth, but for the truth.

Kikuyu: Tũkoragwo na gĩkeno rĩrĩa rĩothe tũtarĩ na hinya, no inyuĩ mũrĩ na hinya; na nĩtũmũhooyagĩra mũtuĩke andũ aagĩrĩru kũna.
English: For we rejoice when we are weak and you are strong. We also pray for this: your becoming perfect.

Kikuyu: Gĩtũmi kĩrĩa kĩratũma nyandĩke maũndũ maya o na itarĩ hamwe na inyuĩ-rĩ, nĩgeetha hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ngooka ndikaanahũthĩre ũhoti wakwa ũũru, ũhoti ũrĩa Mwathani aaheire ũrĩ wa kũmũkũria, no ti wa kũmũthũkia.
English: For this cause I write these things while absent, that I may not deal sharply when present, according to the authority which the Lord gave me for building up and not for tearing down.

Kikuyu: Na rĩu ngĩrĩkia-rĩ, ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, tigwoi na wega. Thiiagai na mĩthiĩre ĩrĩa yagĩrĩire, thikagĩrĩriai ithaithana rĩakwa, gĩagai na meciiria o mamwe, na mũtũũranagie na thayũ. Nake Ngai wa wendo na thayũ nĩarĩkoragwo hamwe na inyuĩ.
English: Finally, brothers, rejoice! Be perfected. Be comforted. Be of the same mind. Live in peace, and the God of love and peace will be with you.

Kikuyu: O mũndũ nĩageithie ũrĩa ũngĩ na ngeithi theru cia kĩmumunyano.
English: Greet one another with a holy kiss.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa othe aamũre nĩmamũgeithia.
English: All the saints greet you.

Kikuyu: Wega wa Mwathani Jesũ Kristũ, na wendani wa Ngai, na ngwatanĩro ya Roho Mũtheru, ĩroikaraga na inyuĩ inyuothe.
English: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, God’s love, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all. Amen.
